phenomenon: selective application of fairness frameworks
claim: agents choose fairness frameworks strategically to maximize personal payoff

phenomenon: gap between judgment and action
claim: ordinary weakness-of-will is a prototype concept shaped by multiple contextual factors, not a unitary phenomenon

phenomenon: peripheral visual intake during sequential processing
claim: preview benefit from distant elements depends on cognitive load from intervening material

phenomenon: resource allocation during public-health crisis
claim: concentrated epidemic burden causes deterioration of non-targeted health services through resource displacement

phenomenon: subjective-well-being trajectories through partnership formation and dissolution
claim: partnership effects on well-being are similar across sexual orientations but modulated by gender and partnership-type

phenomenon: moral judgment of inaction versus action
claim: actors and judges systematically rate harmful inactions as less immoral than equivalent harmful actions because inactions are perceived as non-causal

phenomenon: visual-quantity inference and behavioral adjustment
claim: package visuals showing more units anchor consumption upward regardless of actual quantity through modified quantity judgments

phenomenon: skill acquisition response to structured instruction
claim: comprehensive phonetically-grounded instruction with computer support benefits struggling readers; adaptive features add no marginal gain

phenomenon: neural response to conditioned stimuli paired with self-referent information
claim: neural markers of conditioning emerge under pairing conditions even without conscious stimulus recognition

phenomenon: performance evaluation based on perceptual cues
claim: visual appearance dominates audio quality when evaluating collective performance despite domain-experts ranking sound as primary

phenomenon: sustained economic growth under ethnic diversity
claim: government perceived impartiality enables sustained growth regardless of policy content; fairness perception buffers against ethnic-conflict disruption

phenomenon: manufacturing responsiveness through constraint
claim: simplicity in manufacturing process combined with procedural discipline achieves responsiveness better than traditional trade-offs

phenomenon: memory confidence degradation through stimulus revisiting
claim: repeated engagement with an object (checking or use) reduces memory accuracy, confidence, and detail equally, creating distrust cycle

phenomenon: attribution of knowledge states in uncertain conditions
claim: knowledge attribution increases when epistemic targets perform negative or blameworthy actions, revealing moral judgment's influence on folk-knowledge concepts

phenomenon: coalition behavior under preference heterogeneity
claim: higher preference polarization predicts smaller government in democracies; effect absent in autocracies

phenomenon: civic engagement following traumatic experience
claim: crime victimization increases political participation dramatically; effect size rivals education but sometimes shifts participation toward authoritarianism

phenomenon: norm-driven collective behavior
claim: local prevalence of emancipative values amplifies and elevates individuals' own values' effect on protest participation

phenomenon: transnational political identity and solidarity
claim: police violence against diaspora co-ethnics abroad directly undermines local institutional trust; effect concentrated in co-ethnic observers

phenomenon: substance use and altered-perception risk
claim: cigarette smoking is as strong a predictor of psychotic-like experiences as cannabis; cannabis association is confounded by smoking

phenomenon: institutional trust under perceived demographic threat
claim: perceived immigration threat reduces political trust independently; effect is mediated by historical immigration experience
